dimensions.name: Time Spent on Page - Bucketed
dimensions.description: The amount of time a visitor spends on the page. The amount of time is categorized into different time ranges or "buckets." This can help you understand how long visitors interact with a given metric on the site.

dimensions.name: Bot Name
dimensions.description: Shows the names of the bots detected or custom bot rules supplied. Bot Name should only be used with Bot Page Views and Bot Occurrences. If this dimensions is used with any other Analytics metric, no data will result because it is unassociated with normal customer-based data in Analytics. Data processing for Bot Page Views began between February 26 and March 10, 2023, any reporting window prior to this date will not have data. This is an Adobe provided component.

dimensions.name: Browser
dimensions.description: Shows the name and version of the browser used to access the site. This can help you prioritize which browsers and browser versions you use when testing new features or versions of your site.

dimensions.name: Browser Height - Granular
dimensions.description: Categorizes site visitors based on the height of their browser window.

dimensions.name: Browser Height - Bucketed
dimensions.description: Shows the height of the browser window, classified in groups of 100 pixels. This can help you prioritize content to make it visible to users without scrolling.

dimensions.name: Browser Type
dimensions.description: Shows the name of browser providers used to access the site (such as Google and Apple). This can help you prioritize which browsers you use when testing new features or versions of your site.

dimensions.name: Browser Width - Granular
dimensions.description: Shows the width of the browser window, classified in groups of 100 pixels.

dimensions.name: Browser Width - Bucketed
dimensions.description: Shows the width of the browser window, classified in groups of 100 pixels. This can help you optimize content for viewing.

dimensions.name: Category
dimensions.description: The product category of the hit. If your implementation uses the 'products' variable, this can help you see metrics about the product category, such as top sellers or most viewed.

dimensions.name: ClickMap Action (Legacy)
dimensions.description: Identifies the type of click that a visitor made on a specific element of a web page. This dimension is used in conjunction with the "click map" report in Adobe Analytics, which provides a visual representation of where visitors are clicking on a web page. The "click map action" dimension can have a number of different values, depending on the type of element that was clicked on. For example, some common values might include "link click", "button click", "form submit", "video play", or "image click".

dimensions.name: Color Depth
dimensions.description: The number of colors that a screen supports

dimensions.name: Connection Type
dimensions.description: The method used by a visitor to connect to the internet, such as a wired or wireless connection. This can help you know how to optimize site content based on visitors' connection speeds.

dimensions.name: Cookie Support
dimensions.description: Whether a visitor's browser supports cookies for a given hit. This can help you determine the ratio of visitors who use browsers that support cookies, and those who intentionally disable them.

dimensions.name: Customer Loyalty
dimensions.description: The degree to which visitors consistently engage with and return to the site over time. This can help you understand how the site affects purchasing behavior.

dimensions.name: Custom Link
dimensions.description: Shows the names of custom links on your site. This can help you understand the types of links visitors most often click.

dimensions.name: Daily Unique Customers
dimensions.description: The number of unique visitors per day.

dimensions.name: Hour
dimensions.description: The hour that a given metric occurred (rounded down). This can help you see metrics over time in trended reports.

dimensions.name: Minute
dimensions.description: The minute that a given metric occurred (rounded down). This can help you see metrics over time in trended reports.

dimensions.name: Month
dimensions.description: The month that a given metric occurred. This can help you see metrics over time in trended reports.

dimensions.name: Quarter
dimensions.description: The quarter that a given metric occurred. This can help you see metrics over time in trended reports.

dimensions.name: Week
dimensions.description: The week that a given metric occurred. This can help you see metrics over time in trended reports.

dimensions.name: Year
dimensions.description: The year that a given metric occurred.

dimensions.name: Days Before First Purchase
dimensions.description: The number of days between a user's first visit to a website and their first purchase.

dimensions.name: Days Since Last Purchase
dimensions.description: The number of days between a visitor's current visit and their most recent purchase. This can help you understand visitor behavior after making a purchase on the site.

dimensions.name: Days Since Last Visit
dimensions.description: The number of days between a visitor's current visit and their most recent visit. This can help you understand visitor behavior after visiting the site.

dimensions.name: Domains
dimensions.description: Access points that visitors use to access the internet.

dimensions.name: Download Link
dimensions.description: The names of download links on the site. This can help you understand things like which files are downloaded most frequently and whether visitors download different file types if offered.

dimensions.name: Entry Page
dimensions.description: The names of pages on your site. This can help you understand which pages on your site perform the best. This is one of the most commonly used dimensions.

dimensions.name: Entry Page Original
dimensions.description: The first page viewed by a visitor when they enter the site.

dimensions.name: Entry Site Section
dimensions.description: Lists the names of site sections on your site. For large sites, it is helpful to group pages into sections. This dimension is useful to see the top-viewed or top-performing site sections.

dimensions.name: Exit Link
dimensions.description: The names of links that leads visitors away from the site. This can help you understand which links are most popular that point to domains outside of your site.

dimensions.name: Exit Page
dimensions.description: The names of pages on your site. This can help you understand which pages on your site perform the best. This is one of the most commonly used dimensions.

dimensions.name: Exit Site Section
dimensions.description: Lists the names of site sections on your site. For large sites, it is helpful to group pages into sections. This dimension is useful to see the top-viewed or top-performing site sections.

dimensions.name: First Touch Channel
dimensions.description: The first marketing channel a visitor matches with during their engagement period. This can help you understand which marketing channels drive initial traffic to the site.

dimensions.name: First Touch Channel Detail
dimensions.description: The first marketing channel a visitor matches with during their engagement period. This can help you understand what contributed to the hit matching a marketing channel.

dimensions.name: Cities
dimensions.description: The geographic location of a visitor at the city level.

dimensions.name: Countries
dimensions.description: The geographic location of a visitor at the country level.

dimensions.name: US DMA
dimensions.description: The geographic location of a visitor at the Designated Market Area (DMA) level.

dimensions.name: Regions
dimensions.description: The geographic location of a visitor at the region level.

dimensions.name: Hit Datetime
dimensions.description: The date and time of a visitor's interaction with the site.

dimensions.name: Hit Depth
dimensions.description: How far into a visit a given hit is. This can help you understand how long it takes for visitors to perform actions on the site.

dimensions.name: Hit Type
dimensions.description: Shows whether a mobile app was in the foreground or background when the hit was sent to Adobe data collection servers.

dimensions.name: Java Enabled
dimensions.description: Determines if a visitor's browser at the time of a visit has Java enabled. This is helpful if you want to introduce Java-based functionality on the site but you aren't sure how many visitors already have Java enabled.

dimensions.name: Language
dimensions.description: A visitor's preferred language as defined in the browser. This can aid in localization efforts by helping you understand the languages most preferred by visitors.

dimensions.name: Last Touch Channel
dimensions.description: The most recent marketing channel a visitor matches with during that visitor's engagement period. This can help you understand which marketing channels drive traffic to your site that result in conversions.

dimensions.name: Last Touch Channel Detail
dimensions.description: Details about the most recent marketing channel a visitor matches with during that visitor's engagement period. This can help you understand what contributed to the hit matching a marketing channel.

dimensions.name: Marketing Channel
dimensions.description: The most recent marketing channel a visitor matches with during that visitor's engagement period. This can help you understand which marketing channels drive traffic to your site that result in conversions. This functions identically to the 'Last Touch Channel.'

dimensions.name: Marketing Channel Detail
dimensions.description: Details about the most recent marketing channel a visitor matches with during that visitor's engagement period. This can help you understand what contributed to the hit matching a marketing channel. This functions identically to the 'Last Touch Channel Detail.'

dimensions.name: Mobile Audio Support
dimensions.description: The file formats the device can play.

dimensions.name: Mobile Max Bookmark Length
dimensions.description: The maximum number of bytes that the mobile device supports in bookmarked URLs. Recent devices typically do not have a limit.

dimensions.name: Mobile Carrier
dimensions.description: The telecommunications company that provides cellular network connectivity to a mobile device.

dimensions.name: Mobile Color Depth
dimensions.description: The color depth of the mobile device, in bits.

dimensions.name: Mobile Cookie Support
dimensions.description: The ability of a mobile device to store and manage browser cookies.

dimensions.name: Mobile Device
dimensions.description: The model of the mobile device used to access the site.

dimensions.name: Mobile Device Number
dimensions.description: Shows if the mobile device transmits its number.

dimensions.name: Mobile Device Type
dimensions.description: The type of mobile device used to access the site (such as phone, tablet, or television).

dimensions.name: Mobile DRM
dimensions.description: The type of DRM the mobile device supports.

dimensions.name: Mobile Max Email Length
dimensions.description: The maximum number of bytes that the mobile device supports in an email. Recent devices typically do not have a limit.

dimensions.name: Mobile Image Support
dimensions.description: The ability of mobile devices that access the site to display and render images on the site.

dimensions.name: Mobile Information Services
dimensions.description: The types of news services supported by the device. Recent devices typically do not report this information.

dimensions.name: Mobile Java VM
dimensions.description: The versions of Java that the device supports.

dimensions.name: Mobile Mail Decoration
dimensions.description: Determines if the device supports Decomail, a feature once popular on Japanese devices.

dimensions.name: Mobile Manufacturer
dimensions.description: The manufacturer of the mobile device used to access the site (such as Apple or Samsung).

dimensions.name: Mobile Net Protocols
dimensions.description: The communication protocols used by mobile devices to access the site (such as Edge, GPRS, UMTS, and LTE).

dimensions.name: Mobile Operating System (deprecated)
dimensions.description: This dimension is deprecated. Use 'Operating system' instead.

dimensions.name: Mobile Push To Talk
dimensions.description: Determines if the device supports PTT (Push to talk), which allows the mobile device to behave similar to a two-way radio. Recent devices typically do not report this feature.

dimensions.name: Mobile Screen Height
dimensions.description: The height of the screen, in pixels.

dimensions.name: Mobile Screen Size
dimensions.description: The full dimensions of the mobile device, in pixels.

dimensions.name: Mobile Screen Width
dimensions.description: The width of the screen, in pixels.

dimensions.name: Mobile Max Browser URL Length
dimensions.description: The maximum number of characters that can be included in the site URL when accessed from a mobile browser.

dimensions.name: Mobile Video Support
dimensions.description: The video file formats and codecs that the mobile device supports.

dimensions.name: Monitor Resolution
dimensions.description: The height and width of the active display in pixels. This can help you prioritize content to make it visible to users without scrolling.

dimensions.name: Monthly Unique Customers
dimensions.description: The number of unique visitors per month.

dimensions.name: Operating Systems
dimensions.description: Provides data on the various desktop and mobile operating systems used by visitors to the site (includes operating system versions). This can help you understand which operating systems are most common if you have features that are supported only on certain operating systems.

dimensions.name: Operating System Types
dimensions.description: Provides data on the various desktop and mobile operating systems used by visitors to the site, regardless of the operating system version. This can help you understand which operating systems are most common if you have features that are supported only on certain operating systems.

dimensions.name: Tracking Opt-out Reason
dimensions.description: This dimension acts as a preview to show you data that would be excluded if you enabled Privacy Settings. This can help you determine if your implementation would be negatively impacted if you enabled Privacy Settings under Report Suite Settings.

dimensions.name: Page
dimensions.description: The names of pages on your site. This can help you understand which pages on your site perform the best. This is one of the most commonly used dimensions.

dimensions.name: Pages Not Found
dimensions.description: Shows URLs that contained an error. This can help you lower the number of errors that visitor get on your site.

dimensions.name: Time Spent on Page - Granular
dimensions.description: The amount of time a visitor spends on the page. This can help you understand how long visitors interact with a given metric on the site.

dimensions.name: Page URL
dimensions.description: The URLs on the site.

dimensions.name: Paid Search
dimensions.description: Compares any metric between paid search and natural search. All other hits outside search engines are omitted. This can help you understand how your paid search efforts compare with organic search.

dimensions.name: Persistent Cookie Support
dimensions.description: Shows if the hit used a visitor identifier that originated from a persistent source. The most common persistent source is from a cookie, but it can also use mobile headers and other sources.

dimensions.name: Product
dimensions.description: Shows the name of the product in the hit. If your implementation uses the products variable, it can help you understand metrics about your products, such as top sellers or most viewed.

dimensions.name: Quarterly Unique Customers
dimensions.description: The number of unique visitors per quarter.

dimensions.name: Referrer
dimensions.description: The website or source that a visitor used to arrive at the site. This can help you understand which specific URLs drive the most traffic to your site.

dimensions.name: Referrer Type
dimensions.description: The generic channels that a visitor used to arrive at the site (such as a search engine or another website).

dimensions.name: Referring Domain
dimensions.description: The domain of the website that a visitor used to arrive at the site. This can help you understand which third-party sites drive the most traffic to yours.

dimensions.name: Return Frequency
dimensions.description: Shows the time between visits from returning visitors. When a visitor returns to your site, it looks at the previous visit's timing and categorizes the current hit accordingly. This can help you gauge your site's appeal and track the impact of your content and promotions over time.

dimensions.name: Search Engine
dimensions.description: The search engine used to arrive at the site through a paid or natural (unpaid) search link.

dimensions.name: Search Keyword
dimensions.description: The words or phrases used when entering the site through a paid or natural (unpaid) search link.

dimensions.name: Search Engine - Natural
dimensions.description: The search engine used when entering the site through an natural (unpaid) search link.

dimensions.name: Search Keyword - Natural
dimensions.description: The words or phrases used when entering the site through a natural (unpaid) search link.

dimensions.name: All Search Page Rank
dimensions.description: The page of search results a visitor clicked through to your site. For example, if your site appears on the second page of a search engine's search results, the dimension item for this variable is "Search Page 2".

dimensions.name: Search Engine - Paid
dimensions.description: The search engine used when entering the site through a paid search link.

dimensions.name: Search Keyword - Paid
dimensions.description: The words or phrases used when entering the site through a paid search link.

dimensions.name: Single Page Visits
dimensions.description: Occurrences when visitors leave the site after viewing only a single page.

dimensions.name: Site Section
dimensions.description: Lists the names of site sections on your site. For large sites, it is helpful to group pages into sections. This dimension is useful to see the top-viewed or top-performing site sections.

dimensions.name: AM/PM
dimensions.description: The time of day (whether AM or PM) when a visitor accessed the site.

dimensions.name: Day of Month
dimensions.description: The number or percentage of visitors who access the site, categorized by month.

dimensions.name: Day of Year
dimensions.description: The numeric day of any given year. This allows you to view data by day, without having to use static dates as dimension items.

dimensions.name: Hour of Day
dimensions.description: The numeric hour of any given day. This allows you to view data by relative time of day, without having to use static hours as dimension items.

dimensions.name: Month of Year
dimensions.description: The month of any given year. This allows you to view data by the month of the year, without having to use a static date as dimension items.

dimensions.name: Quarter of Year
dimensions.description: The quarter of any given year. This allows you to view data by quarter of the year, without having to use static dates as dimension items.

dimensions.name: Weekday/Weekend
dimensions.description: The number or percentage of visitors who access the site, categorized by weekday and weekend.

dimensions.name: Time Prior to Event
dimensions.description: Reports the amount of time that passed between the first hit of the visit and the desired metric. This dimension is useful to determine the amount of time it takes to hit a success event, such as a form submission or a purchase.

dimensions.name: Time Spent per Visit - Bucketed
dimensions.description: Categorizes the length of time that visitors spend on the site during a single visit into different time ranges or "buckets".

dimensions.name: Time Zone
dimensions.description: The time zone in which visitors are located when they interact with the site. This can help you understand user behavior and engagement patterns across different geographic regions.

dimensions.name: SC-TnT
dimensions.description: The base URL of the Adobe Target server that is being used for testing or personalization activities.

dimensions.name: Top Level Domain
dimensions.description: The last part of a website's domain name after the final dot, such as ".com", ".org", or ".edu". This can help you understand how visitors are accessing the site and which top-level domains are driving the most traffic.

dimensions.name: Visit Number
dimensions.description: The number of times a visitor accessed the site within a defined time period. This can help you understand how engaged a visitor is when returning to your site.

dimensions.name: Visitor ID
dimensions.description: A unique, persistent identifier assigned to each individual visitor who accesses the site. This identifier enables Adobe Analytics to track and analyze visitor behavior over time, even if the visitor is using multiple devices or browsers.

dimensions.name: Visit Page Number
dimensions.description: A sequential number that represents the order in which a specific page was viewed by a visitor during a given visit to the site. This can help you understand the path that visitors take as they navigate through the site, and can be used to analyze visitor behavior and engagement.

dimensions.name: Weekly Unique Customers
dimensions.description: The number of unique visitors per week.

dimensions.name: Yearly Unique Customers
dimensions.description: The number of unique visitors per year.

dimensions.name: Zip Code
dimensions.description: Categorizes site visitors based on their zip code or postal code. This can help you understand the success of local advertising or see where in the world your site performs best.

metrics.name: Average Time Spent on Page (seconds)
metrics.description: The average amount of time visitors spend on a page.

metrics.name: Average Time Spent on Site (seconds)
metrics.description: The total amount of time visitors interact with a specific dimension item, per sequence with a dimension item.

metrics.name: Average Visit Depth
metrics.description: The average number of pages viewed during a single visit to the site. This can help you understand how engaged visitors are with content on the site.

metrics.name: Bot Occurrences
metrics.description: The number of hits where a bot traffic was detected. The Bot Occurrences metric should only be used with the Bot Name, Page, or standard time dimensions (such as Day, Week, etc). If this metric is used with any other Analytics dimension, no data will result because it is unassociated with normal customer-based data in Analytics. Data processing for Bot Occurrences began between February 26 and March 10, 2023, any reporting window prior to this date will not have data. This is an Adobe provided component.

metrics.name: Bot Page Views
metrics.description: Shows the number of times a bot was set or persisted on a page. The Bot Page Views metric should only be used with the Bot Name, Page, or standard time dimensions (such as Day, Week, etc). If this metric is used with any other Analytics dimension, no data will result because it is unassociated with normal customer-based data in Analytics. Data processing for Bot Page Views began between February 26 and March 10, 2023, any reporting window prior to this date will not have data. This is an Adobe provided component.

metrics.name: Bounce Rate
metrics.description: The ratio of visits that contained exactly one hit compared to the number of visits on that page. This can help you to understand which dimension items have the highest bounce rate, or to see an aggregated total bounce rate of your site over time.

metrics.name: Bounces
metrics.description: The number of times a visitor leaves the site after viewing only one page. This can help you understand the top entry pages that cause visitors to bounce, or to see how bounces trend over time.

metrics.name: Cart Additions
metrics.description: The number of times a visitor added something to their cart. This can help you understand at what part of the conversion funnel that customers show enough interest in a product to add it to their cart.

metrics.name: Cart Removals
metrics.description: The number of times a visitor removes an item from their online shopping cart during a session. This can help you understand which factors might be preventing users from completing a purchase.

metrics.name: Carts
metrics.description: The number of times visitors to the site added items to their online shopping carts.

metrics.name: Cart Views
metrics.description: The number of times a visitor viewed their shopping cart. This can help you understand at what part of the conversion funnel that customers view the contents in their cart.

metrics.name: Checkouts
metrics.description: The number of times visitors to the site initiated the checkout process to purchase a product or service.

metrics.name: Activity Map Link By Region Instances
aliases: Activity Map Link By Region Instances
activity map link by region instances

metrics.name: Download Link Instances
metrics.description: The number of times visitors click on download links on the site. This can help you understand how effective certain links are.

metrics.name: Entries
metrics.description: The number of times visitors to the site first land on a given page to start their session.

metrics.name: Exit Link Instances
metrics.description: The number of times visitors clicked a specific link before leaving your website. This can help you understand which links are causing users to leave and potentially identify opportunities for optimization.

metrics.name: Exits
metrics.description: The number of times a given dimension item is captured as the last value in a visit. This can help you understand the last thing visitors see before leaving your site, allowing you to optimize the experience a visitor gets before they leave.

metrics.name: Total Seconds Spent
metrics.description: The amount of time visitors spend viewing a specific item on the site, such as a product or piece of content. This can help you understand how interested visitors are in specific products.

metrics.name: New Engagements
metrics.description: the number of times a visitor matches a marketing channel for the first time in that visitor's engagement period. This can compare any dimension with a visitor matching a marketing channel processing rule for the first time.

metrics.name: Occurrences
metrics.description: The number of hits where a given dimension was set or persisted.

metrics.name: Orders
metrics.description: The total number of purchase events made on your site. This metric is vital for eCommerce sites in measuring conversion because you can combine this metric with any dimension to see which dimension items contributed to an order.

metrics.name: Orders per Visit
metrics.description: The ratio of orders that visitors made over the total number of visits to your site. This can help you understand conversion rate for your site.

metrics.name: Page Events
metrics.description: Specific user interactions or actions that occur on the site, such as clicks, form submissions, video plays, downloads, and other custom events.

metrics.name: Pages Not Found
metrics.description: Shows URLs that contained an error. This can help you lower the number of errors that visitor get on your site.

metrics.name: Page Views
metrics.description: Shows the number of times a given dimension item was set or persisted on a page. It is one of the most common and basic metrics in reports.

metrics.name: Product Views
metrics.description: The number of times any product was viewed. This can help you see your top-viewed products, or how total product views trend over time.

metrics.name: Reloads
metrics.description: The number of times a dimension item was present during a reload. A visitor refreshing their browser is the most common way to trigger a reload.

metrics.name: Revenue
metrics.description: The monetary amount of products purchased within all orders. This metric is vital for eCommerce sites in measuring conversion because you can combine this metric with any dimension to see which dimension items contributed to revenue.

metrics.name: Searches
metrics.description: The number of hits that match Adobe's external search detection. This can help you understand how non-search dimensions contributed to search engine traffic.

metrics.name: Single Page Visits
metrics.description: Occurrences when visitors leave the site after viewing only a single page.

metrics.name: Activity Conversions
metrics.description: The number of times visitors complete a specific action on your website, which is defined as a "conversion."

metrics.name: Time Spent per Visit (seconds)
metrics.description: The average amount of time that visitors interact with a given dimension item during each visit.

metrics.name: Time Spent per Visitor (seconds)
metrics.description: The average amount of time that visitors interact with a given dimension item during a visitor's entire lifetime.

metrics.name: Units
metrics.description: The total number of products purchased within all orders. This metric is vital for eCommerce sites in measuring conversion because you can combine this metric with any dimension to see which dimension items contributed to the number of products purchased.

metrics.name: Unique Visitors
metrics.description: The number of unique individuals who have visited the site during a specified time period. This can help you understand the overall reach of the site.

metrics.name: Daily Unique Visitors
metrics.description: The number of unique visitors to the site during each day in a given time period.

metrics.name: Hourly Unique Visitors
metrics.description: The number of unique visitors to the site during each hour of the day. This can help you understand the time of day when the site is busiest to help optimize marketing campaigns and website content.

metrics.name: Visitors with Experience Cloud ID
metrics.description: The number of unique visitors to the site based on their Marketing Cloud Visitor ID.

metrics.name: Monthly Unique Visitors
metrics.description: The number of unique visitors to the site during each month in a given time period.

metrics.name: Quarterly Unique Visitors
metrics.description: The number of unique visitors to the site during each quarter in a given time period.

metrics.name: Weekly Unique Visitors
metrics.description: The number of unique visitors to the site during each week in a given time period.

metrics.name: Yearly Unique Visitors
metrics.description: The number of unique visitors to the site during each year in a given time period.

metrics.name: Visits
metrics.description: The number of sessions across all visitors on the site.

segments.name: USA
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from the United States.

segments.name: 11-20 Days
segments.description: Visitors to the site whose last visit was between 11 and 20 days ago.

segments.name: 21-50 Days
segments.description: Visitors to the site whose last visit was between 21 and 50 days ago.

segments.name: Chrome Browsers
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from a Chrome browser.

segments.name: 51-100 Days
segments.description: Visitors to the site whose last visit was between 51 and 100 days ago.

segments.name: Visits from Mobile Devices
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from a mobile device.

segments.name: Single Page Visits
segments.description: Visitors who viewed only 1 page during their visit.

segments.name: Mobile Web Hits
segments.description: The number of visits to the website from a mobile device.

segments.name: All Visits
segments.description: All visitors to the site.

segments.name: Co-op regions
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from either the United States or Canada.

segments.name: 0 Days
segments.description: Visitors to the site whose last visit was less than 1 day ago.

segments.name: 100+ Days
segments.description: Visitors to the site whose last visit was more than 100 days ago.

segments.name: Visits from Search Engines
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site through a link result that was returned from a search engine.

segments.name: Visits from Non-Mobile Devices
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from a device that is not a mobile device.

segments.name: 1-5 Days
segments.description: Visitors to the site whose last visit was between 1 and 5 days ago.

segments.name: Users returning after 7 days
segments.description: Visitors who returned to the site after an absence of 7 or more days.

segments.name: Non-Single Page Visit (Non-Bounces)
segments.description: Visitors who viewed more than 1 page during their visit.

segments.name: Co-op unsupported regions
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from anywhere outside the United States or Canada.

segments.name: Visits from Natural Search
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site through a natural search link (not a paid search link).

segments.name: Purchasers
segments.description: Visitors to the site who made a purchase.

segments.name: First Time Visits
segments.description: Visitors who visited the site only 1 time.

segments.name: iOS
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from Apple iOS devices, such as iPhones and iPads.

segments.name: Canada
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from Canada.

segments.name: Visits with a Referrer
segments.description: Visitors who arrived at the site from another website or source.

segments.name: Mobile Hits
segments.description: The number of visits to the site from a mobile device.

segments.name: Non-Purchasers
segments.description: Identifies visitors who have never completed a purchase, using visitor-level exclusion logic to capture all users without any order events in their profile.

segments.name: Visits with Visitor ID Cookie
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site and can be identified by a unique visitor ID cookie.

segments.name: Viewed Product didn't Add to Cart
segments.description: Visitors who viewed a product without adding it to their cart.
aliases: Viewed Product didn't Add to Cart
viewed product didn't add to cart

segments.name: Visits from Phones
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from a mobile phone rather than another type of mobile device.

segments.name: Media Content Type: VoD
segments.description: Visitors who interacted specifically with Video on Demand (VoD) content while visiting the site.

segments.name: Media Content Type: Audiobook
segments.description: Visitors who interacted specifically with audiobook content while visiting the site.

segments.name: Media Stream Type: Audio
segments.description: Visitors who interacted specifically with audio content while visiting the site.

segments.name: Visits from Social Sites
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site through a link from a social media platform.

segments.name: Media Content Type: Podcast
segments.description: Visitors who interacted specifically with podcast content while visiting the site.

segments.name: Media Stream Type: Video
segments.description: Visitors who interacted specifically with video content while visiting the site.

segments.name: Has an Action
segments.description: Visitors who have completed a certain action on the site, such as making a purchase or completing a form.

segments.name: Mobile App Hits
segments.description: The number of visits to the mobile app from the mobile device.

segments.name: Abandon Cart
segments.description: Visitors who added items to their cart but didn't complete the purchase.

segments.name: People who have not had a Crash
segments.description: Visitors who used a mobile device to access the site and who did not experience a crash while visiting the site.
aliases: People who have not had a Crash
people who have not had a crash

segments.name: ITP Visitors (ITP 2.1, Non-CNAME implementations)
segments.description: Visitors with browsers that are affected by ITP 2.1. Specifically, starting with any browsers on iOS 12.2 as well Safari 12.1. ITP 2.1 applied to implementations where Adobe cookies were set as first-party using JavaScript. This did not impact implementations using a CNAME to set server-side first party cookies. CNAME implementations were impacted in November 2020 when ITP was extended to CNAME implementations.

segments.name: Paid Search
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site through a paid search link.

segments.name: Media Content Type: Linear
segments.description: Visitors who interacted specifically with linear media content while visiting the site, such as live streaming or broadcast TV.

segments.name: Media Stream Type: All
segments.description: Visitors who interacted specifically with any type of media content while visiting the site.

segments.name: Visits from Campaign
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site through a specific marketing campaign.

segments.name: 6-10 Days
segments.description: Visitors to the site whose last visit was between 6 and 10 days ago.

segments.name: Return Visits
segments.description: Visitors who visited the site more than 1 time.

segments.name: Android
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from Android devices, such as Android phones and tablets.

segments.name: ITP Visitors (Nov 2020 Update for CNAME)
segments.description: If you are setting first-party cookies using a CNAME implementation, this segment will capture which users are on affected browsers. Specifically Safari on Big Sur or all browsers on iOS 14.2+. NOTE: Currently (May 2020) for all major browsers the user agent string incorrectly represents Mac OS 11.x as Mac OS 10.x. This makes it impossible to determine if user is using Mac OS 11 and thus subject to the change in ITP to apply to CNAME implementations. This segment will be affected by this inaccuracy. At this point it is unclear when or if this will be corrected. In November of 2020, ITP policy was updated to apply to implementations where cookies were set via a CNAME.

segments.name: Non-ITP Visitors (Nov 2020 Update for CNAME)
segments.description: If you are setting first-party cookies using a CNAME implementation, this segment will filter out non-ITP Visitors. Giving a clear view of metrics like "Unique Visitors". In November of 2020, ITP policy was updated to apply to implementations where cookies were set via a CNAME.

segments.name: Media Content Type: Live
segments.description: Visitors who interacted specifically with live content while visiting the site.

segments.name: Non-ITP Visitors (ITP 2.1, Non-CNAME implementations)
segments.description: Visitors with browsers that are affected by ITP 2.1. Specifically starting with any browsers on iOS 12.2 as well Safari 12.1. ITP 2.1 applied to implementations where Adobe cookies were set as first-party using JavaScript. This did not impact implementations using a CNAME to set server-side first party cookies. CNAME implementations were impacted in November 2020 when ITP was extended to CNAME implementations.

segments.name: Web Hits
segments.description: The number of visits to the site from a device that is not a mobile device.

segments.name: Visits from Tablets
segments.description: Visitors who accessed the site from a tablet rather than another type of mobile device.

segments.name: People who have had a Crash
segments.description: Visitors to the site who experienced a website crash while visiting the site.
aliases: People who have had a Crash
people who have had a crash

segments.name: Visits with a Tracking Code
segments.description: The number of visitors who arrived at the site through a specific tracking code or parameter.

segments.name: Media Content Type: AoD
segments.description: Visitors who interacted specifically with Advertising on Demand (AoD) content while visiting the site.
